config.title: Free Walking Tour Tipping FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers about fair tips, cash, cards and what free really means on a Berlin walking tour.

items.q: How much should I tip on a free walking tour in Berlin?
items.a: **For a good 2 hours tour, EUR 10 per person is the minimum fair tip.** EUR 15 is strong, and EUR 20+ is generous.

items.q: Is a free walking tour really free?
items.a: There is no fixed upfront price. The tour is tip-based, which means you join without prepaying and decide at the end what the experience was worth to you.

items.q: Do I need cash for a free walking tour tip?
items.a: Cash is the easiest option in Berlin. Some guides may accept card or PayPal, but you should not rely on that unless the tour page says so clearly.

items.q: Is EUR 5 too little for a free walking tour?
items.a: For a good 2 hours tour, EUR 5 is below the fair minimum. If your budget is genuinely tight or the tour was only okay, give what you can without embarrassment.

items.q: Should children tip on a free walking tour?
items.a: For small children, usually no. For teenagers who fully join the tour like adults, many guests tip a smaller amount or include them in the family total.

items.q: What if I did not enjoy the tour?
items.a: Then tip less or nothing. The point of a tip-based tour is that the guide earns the tip through the quality of the experience.
